weeklyPriorities: Video taping on every practice session
Remember to stand on forefoot for game to initiate footwork
1st serve accuracy and consistency → especially at T
Backhand volley → hold racket longer, keep racket in the front, look at the ball, push more with left feet
High ball handling → practice with ball machine, can be going back or take on rise or overhead
Overhead → practice with wall to find better contact point
Strategy → build hitting pattern for game

weeklyOverview.focus: Consistency, volley, approach shots and high lobs, shots varieties, serves, overhead*
weeklyOverview.drill: Rally, double games; switching between ground stroke and slices; Combination feed → Forehand high, backhand high, approach shot, volley; volley feed from baseline; overhead feed from baseline

sessions.label: 2026-06-07 Sun
sessions.context: Rally w/ Seth
good: Second serve is consistent w/ 1 double fault
Return angle is accurate to the server’s corner
2 good volley with back slice
Volley forehand gets more push from foot, racket keep in the front
bad: High volley → late and out
Backhand needs to prepare higher to drive through the ball
Forehand not enough of unit turn → and late for the fast balls’
Look closer to the ball movement
Also need to drop racket head more to generate spin and power
Stand too close to fast backhand slices from Seth
Not enough variety in games
Overhead still not getting into the right position → move back even more before hitting
Insights
Serving and return of serve is VERY important in a game!
Moving back and forth with the ball when standing at the net for double games
Backhand → turn body more and hold the racket longer to guide volley

sessions.label: 2026-06-06 Sat
sessions.context: Game w/ Alpine hill → Win 6:4/6:4; Serving and volley feeding from baseline w/ Lili
good: 2 double fault in the entire game.
2nd serve consistency with speed
Stable and calm on important points
bad: Not confident enough for 1st serve
Adjusted too slow to the windy condition
Too late for short balls..
Backhand on highballs returns short
Mid court shots goes out mostly → needs more practice with ball machine
Not going back enough for high lob balls
Not enough variety in games
Not moving enough in game
1st serve pinpoint → struggle with toss and hitting alignment
Tossing is not consistent → needs to remove the shoulder movement
Struggle with short ball feeds → late or out
Insights
Needs to play/practice more in wind condition
Aim more safe in the box when windy
Lean forward and prepare racket higher for mid court high balls
When in mid court return w/ good angle and as deep as possible
Needs to practice short balls more → should start with standing in mid court first, then move back to baseline

sessions.label: 2026-05-31 Sun
sessions.context: Double game with Anna, Kimo and Gary. Me & Anna lost 1:6/1:6
good: Return angle on Gary’s serve went well
Backhand stability increased - need to move back more before hitting
bad: 1st serves - all out..
2 double faults in one game
Distracted and not focus enough when balls lob over net person. - need to always prepare! And assume balls are coming to me!!! Same as when at the net.
Do not do drop shot for high balls!! - too risky and not worth it.
Overhead - need to move more be higher the ball and hit up?!
Volley - backhand!!! do NOT swing racket..
Forehand baseline angle was not good enough
Insight
Toss ball more to the front for 1st serve and do not rush it! Shadow swing before game begins
When double faulted, breath and restart or go bounce balls to reset

sessions.label: 2026-05-30 Sat
sessions.context: Practice with Coach
bad: Insight: needs to relax and maintain the same speed to increase consistency!! Use 70%-80% speed for all rallies

sessions.label: 2026-05-30 Sat
sessions.context: USTA single game won 6:4/6:1
good: Winning!
Serve: only 2 double faults, 3 aces with first serve (when I was leading 4:1)
Backhand: more stable and can take the balls on the rise
Corse court slice: good placement and low bounce
bad: High lobs treatment: especially on backhand, my return is too weak and not deep enough, need to prepare higher.
High lob forehand: prepare higher and take the ball down at peak, or go further back to hit the ball in falling..
Too many unforced errors for killer shots…
Weakness: backhand - down the line, forehand - crosscourt too longer
Need to change strategy faster - she was apparently better on backhand from warmup.. then try not to give to her backhand!! Cost 2 sets before changing strategy
Do not hesitate when moving towards the net! It’s better now, but still sometimes missed the best timing
Insights
Need to be more assertive for when opportunities rise - practice put away shots
Returning on week serves with good placement - find better angle than use power, try not to return back to server

sessions.label: 2026-05-04 Mon
sessions.context: 1ST USTA 3.0 game
bad: Overhead!!! - ball machine till conquer it!
Short ball treatment
Lobs hit in the air and better aim
learning: Second serve can be detrimental. Don’t risk it when not needed to
Use drop shots wisely and keep it short
For short balls hit it deeper… do not need to go for hard angles
Need to move for all shots! Especially when my shots’ quality were high! Expect it to come back
Backspin is interesting skill to learn for lobs
Swing opponent or make them move is easier to find loophoes
Lob + drop shot/ backspin shot can be a weapon when used right.
Backhand lob needs improvement

sessions.label: 2026-05-03 Sun
sessions.context: Practice w/ coach; Single game with Bingsen
bad: Adjust with more steps before hitting the ball
Recover to position
Follow the ball
Move back when seeing potential lobs
Add a bit down slice for volley to avoid out.
Think of the potential return if i am in the same situation then make decision!
Change pace when possible
Watch double games!
learning: Slow down the racket! Move more on the feet!
first 2 sets in a game should be testing the opponent — learn the weakness and find the patter ASAP!

sessions.label: 2026-05-02 SAT
sessions.context: Double game w/ coach, YJ, Robert
bad: Adjust with more steps before hitting the ball
Recover to position
Follow the ball
Move back when seeing potential lobs
Add a bit down slice for volley to avoid out.
Think of the potential return if i am in the same situation then make decision!
Change pace when possible
Watch double games!
learning: Adjust with small steps to get to the right distance to the ball!
When partner lobs over start to move back → Do not stay in the service box → become an easy target
Move further behind the net to allow more reaction time for volleys
Can hit the ball then call out as well!!! So always hit it after landed!
Do not stay in the middle of the court!! Recover to position once finish the previous shot and keep on toes!
Look at the opponent’s racket!!
Keep focusing on the ball even when partner is approaching it just in case.
Always follow the ball
Communication is important in doubles. Need to coordinate and align on strategies for serving and positions → watch some doubles game!
For slow balls or drop shots → try slice to the close diagonal corner if can
Volley → Hit it in first! Then everything else ..
Lob doesn’t help much with advanced players, angles and ball placement is more important even more than speed
Changing pace can be surprising and disruptive even for advanced players

sessions.label: 2026-05-01 FRI
sessions.context: practice w/ Mo?
bad: Ball catch on the wall
Leg strength + footwork
Slow down swing
learning: Swing slower to allow time to fix eyes on the ball
Footwork is based on anticipation → look at the opponent’s racket
Be prepared even when hitting a good shot
Think ahead at least 1 step before game mode on, and be ready at the right position
Need to practice more hitting to opponent’s backhand
Return/ hit from my most comfortable position, do not take unnecessary risks
When in disadvantage or desperate, go for it! But think of why getting into that situation afterwards
Think of how to dictate the ball direction, plan 2 steps ahead.
Be stable / patience till the opportunity rises
